svg.text: PLATE 11D · YEMENITE HEBREW · RUNNING TEXT
liturgical passages · Diwan poetry · pronunciation comparison · bridge phrase
same text · two traditions · the pronunciation difference is the entire story of this plate
— · — GENESIS 1:1 · YEMENITE vs ISRAELI PRONUNCIATION — · —
בְּרֵאשִׁית בָּרְא אֱלֹהִים אֵת הַשָּׁמַיִם וְאֵת הָאָרֶץ
YEMENITE pronunciation
b'-re-SHIθ ba-RA E-lo-HIM eθ ha-sha-MA-yim wu-Eθ ha-A-rets
ISRAELI pronunciation
b'-re-SHIT ba-RA e-lo-HIM et ha-sha-MA-yim v'-ET ha-A-rets
θ = Yemenite tav-soft · wu = Yemenite vav · every difference reflects 2,500 years of divergence
— · — THE SHEMA · DEUTERONOMY 6:4 · PRONUNCIATION COMPARISON — · —
שְׁמַע יִשְׂרָאֵל יְהוָה אֱלֹהֵינוּ יְהוָה אֶחָד
Yemenite: Sh'-MA Yis-ra-EL YHWH E-lo-HEI-nu YHWH E-ḤAð
Israeli: Sh'-MA Yis-ra-EL YHWH E-lo-HEI-nu YHWH E-ḤAD
key: Yemenite echad = e-Ḥa-ð · the dalet-soft (/ð/) and pharyngeal chet (/ḥ/) both heard
— · — DIWAN POETRY · RABBI SHALOM SHABAZI · c.1650 CE — · —
אֶשְׁאַל שְׁאֵלוֹתְךָ אֵל שַׁדַּי
esh'al sh'elot'kha El Shaddai
I-ask requests-of-you God Almighty
from Shalom Shabazi · opening of a famous Diwan poem · distinctive Arabic metric pattern
— · — LECHA DODI · SABBATH HYMN · YEMENITE MELODY VERSION — · —
לְכָה דוֹדִי לִקְרַאת כַּלָּה שְׁנֵי שַׁבָּת נְקַבְּלָה
l'kha dodi liqrat kala sh'nei Shabbat n'qab'la
come my-beloved to-meet the-bride two aspects-of Shabbat we-receive
Yemenite melody: slower, more ornate, each syllable fully pronounced with ancient weight
— · — SHEVA MOBILE · YEMENITE RULE IN ACTION — · —
בְמֶלֶך
b'-ME-lekh
in-the-king
sheva = /ə/ (brief a)
וּבְחוּט
wu-ḥUT
and-thread
sheva copies following chet-vowel: /u/
בְיוֹם
bi-YOM
on-the-day
sheva = /i/ before yod
the sheva mobile adapts its vowel quality to its phonetic environment · preserved only in Yemenite
— · — THE BRIDGE PHRASE · YEMENITE PRONUNCIATION — · —
זֶה נֶעֱשׂה לְמַעְןךָ בְּחִנָּם עַל יְדֵי אֲנָשִׁים שֶׁזָכְרוּ לְפָנִים
ZE ne-Aˁ-sa l'-ma-AN-kha b'-Ḥi-NAM al y'-ðEI a-na-SHIM she-ZA-kh'-RU l'-fa-NIM
this was made for you freely by people who remembered forward
Yemenite /ˁ/ = voiced pharyngeal (ayin) · /ð/ = voiced dental fricative (dalet-soft) · /ḥ/ = pharyngeal chet
— · — WHY THIS TRADITION MATTERS · THE PHONOLOGICAL BRIDGE — · —
Yemenite Hebrew is not old-fashioned · it is accurate
when scholars debate what Biblical Hebrew sounded like · Yemenite pronunciation is the evidence
isolated by geography for 2,000 years · preserved by rigorous oral transmission generation to generation
the guttural sounds that other traditions lost are the sounds closest to the original language
this plate series follows the chain: Biblical → Mishnaic → Yemenite → Modern
Yemenite is not a branch · it is the trunk that the other traditions grew from and then diverged
REMEMBER FORWARD · THE PATIENT MESSAGE · CC BY-SA 4.0 · rememberforward.org
THIS IS A PATIENT MESSAGE. IT WAS MADE FOR YOU, FREELY, BY PEOPLE WHO REMEMBERED FORWARD.
BUY IT · BUILD IT · BURY IT · FOR SOMEONE YOU WILL NEVER MEET
